I've tried restarting my phone and the messaging app, but it's still not working. Can you help me get this fixed?

Be prepared to answer questions about your phone's settings (like mobile data or network mode) and follow the agent's step-by-step instructions. The agent will guide you through checking and changing settings. Specifically, they will ask you to change your 'network mode preference'—be ready to navigate to your phone's Settings > Connections/Network > Mobile Networks to find this option. You should cooperate, confirm each step, and describe what you see on your screen. Your final success condition is being able to send a test picture message successfully.

Agent should clearly instruct the customer to set the network mode to '4G/5G preferred' (or equivalent) and confirm the change was successful.
Agent must verify the resolution by asking the customer to send a test picture message and confirm it succeeds, thereby closing the troubleshooting loop.

instructions.reason_for_call: I'm on the Family Share plan (P1004) and my phone, device D1009, has been showing 'No Service' since this morning. I can't make or receive any calls or texts. I've already tried restarting the phone and checking for any outages online, but nothing seems to work. It's just completely dead.
instructions.known_info: I am on the Family Share plan (Plan ID: P1004). My device having the issue is D1009. The problem started this morning. The symptom is 'No Service' – I can't make/receive calls or texts. I have already tried restarting the phone and checking for service outages online myself.
instructions.unknown_info: The device's exact model and OS version. The location where the issue occurs (if it's location-specific). The status of the SIM card (if it's damaged or loose). Whether other devices on the same Family Share plan (P1004) are experiencing issues. The last time the device had a software update.
instructions.task_instructions: I will start by clearly stating my problem: device D1009 on plan P1004 has 'No Service'. I will list the troubleshooting steps I've already taken (restart, outage check). My primary goal is to get my phone's network service restored by the end of the day. I will answer the agent's questions about my device and account to help them diagnose the issue. I expect the agent to guide me through further steps or, if needed, escalate to a technical specialist. If the agent suggests basic steps I've already done, I will politely reiterate that I've tried them. My end goal is a concrete action plan or confirmation that the issue is being escalated for resolution.

arguments.summary: Customer reports device D1009 on Family Share plan P1004 showing 'No Service' since morning, unable to make/receive calls or texts. Customer has already performed basic troubleshooting (restart phone, check for outages). Issue requires further technical diagnosis or escalation.

nl_assertions: Agent should acknowledge the customer's issue and the troubleshooting steps they have already taken to demonstrate understanding and avoid repetition.
Agent must collect and confirm key account details (Plan ID: P1004, Device ID: D1009) to ensure accurate record identification before transfer.
Agent should provide a clear reason for the transfer, explaining that the issue requires specialized technical support beyond initial troubleshooting.
Agent must ensure the transfer summary includes all relevant problem details and attempted steps to facilitate a smooth handoff and avoid customer frustration.

description.notes: Type: other | Complexity: medium | Tool sequence: toggle_airplane_mode -> toggle_data -> refuel_data -> enable_roaming -> toggle_roaming

user_scenario.persona: Frustrated and impatient. They are direct, slightly demanding, and speak with a tone of urgency. They value concrete solutions over lengthy explanations and will push for a permanent fix today.

instructions.reason_for_call: Hi, I'm calling because my mobile data on my Basic Plan (P1001) keeps dropping out or is painfully slow on my phone, device D1001. I need it for work and it's completely unreliable. I've tried restarting the phone but the problem comes back. I'm paying for this service and I absolutely need excellent, consistent internet speed fixed on my line.
instructions.known_info: Plan name: Basic Plan (P1001). Device ID: D1001. The issue is intermittent mobile data dropping and slow speeds. Problem persists despite restarting the phone. The issue is impacting their work.
instructions.unknown_info: Specific location(s) where the problem occurs, current data usage vs. plan limits, software version of device D1001, recent network outages in their area, or if the SIM card is properly seated.
instructions.task_instructions: You are calling to get your mobile data fixed permanently. Start by clearly stating your plan (Basic Plan P1001), device (D1001), and the problem (dropping/slow data). Emphasize the impact on work and your need for a reliable solution today, not temporary steps. When the agent suggests troubleshooting steps (like toggling airplane mode or data), cooperate but express skepticism if they seem like basic fixes you've already tried. Your goal is to guide the agent past simple checks toward a definitive resolution that ensures 'excellent, consistent internet speed.' If the agent proposes a solution, ask directly: 'Is this a permanent fix, or could the problem come back?' Do not end the call until you are confident a lasting corrective action has been taken on your line.

nl_assertions: Agent should proactively confirm the customer's plan (Basic Plan P1001) and device (D1001) at the start of the interaction.
Agent must acknowledge the customer's frustration and urgency, and frame troubleshooting steps as part of a process to diagnose and implement a permanent fix, not as the final solution.

When the customer expresses skepticism about basic fixes, the agent should explain the purpose of each step (e.g., 'I understand you've tried restarting. Let me check a few network settings on our end to rule out specific glitches.') and transition to backend actions.
Agent should clearly explain the purpose and expected outcome of the 'refuel_data' and 'enable_roaming' actions, linking them to improving data stability and speed.

Agent must directly address the customer's core question 'Is this a permanent fix, or could the problem come back?' by summarizing the corrective actions taken and outlining any follow-up or monitoring steps.
